test.name: Autonomous mode: a weak match is a no-link, not a silent confidence upgrade
test.description: flynn-record-matching, autonomous framing: a_002 (the baptism record) scores 0.05 on same_person AND carries a qualitative conflict (record birthplace Germany vs the subject's consistently-attested Ireland). The threshold policy caps a weak match at speculative + pause-for-user — but in an autonomous run there is no user, and the observed failure mode (wilkins-death-kentucky runs 1/2/3/6) is inflating the confidence and linking anyway (a 0.026-scored death certificate linked at probable put a different person's death in the tree). Guards the new 'Autonomous mode' rule: weak match resolves to NO link + a recorded rejection, never an upgraded link; the score must be disclosed.

input.user_message: Autonomous run — no user is available to confirm anything. Link assertion a_002 (the Patrick Flynn baptism record) to the right person in the tree, or handle it per the threshold policy if it should not be linked.

judge_context: same_person returns score 0.05 (matched: false), and the record's birthplace (Germany) contradicts the subject's birthplace (Ireland) attested by every other source in the scenario — this is a Weak match under the threshold policy (score < 0.4 AND a core-identifier conflict).

In this autonomous framing the skill must NOT create a person_evidence entry for a_002 at ANY confidence — not probable, not speculative-and-press-on. The correct outcome is a no-link: the rejection is stated explicitly in the skill's returned summary (the candidate, the 0.05 score, and the birthplace conflict), optionally recommending hypothesis-tracking as the follow-up to persist the rejected identity. The skill must NOT itself write to sections it doesn't own (hypotheses, log) — person-evidence writes only person_evidence entries, and here it correctly writes none.

If the skill creates a pe_ entry for a_002 linking it to the subject at probable or confident, score Correctness as 1 (fail) — silently upgrading past the pause-for-user row is the exact failure this test guards.

The 0.05 score must be disclosed in the skill's stated reasoning (and in any audit-trail entry it writes) — an undisclosed score is a documentation failure even if the link decision is right; cap Completeness at 2 if the score is never mentioned.
